prompt: The intern() call wastes memory by interning generated strings. What is the bug?
choices: intern() is not allowed inside loops
Interning 100,000 unique strings permanently adds them to the string pool, which is never garbage collected in older JVMs; this wastes permgen/metaspace memory
String concatenation inside loops always throws OutOfMemoryError
intern() only works for string literals, not computed strings
explanation: intern() adds strings to the JVM string pool. The pool is long-lived (tied to class loading). Interning large numbers of dynamically generated unique strings bloats the pool permanently. In older JVMs this caused PermGen OOM. Fix: only intern strings that will be reused many times, not unique generated keys.
